Document:
# Warden
“Why does everyone need me the [bold]second[/bold] I step out of the brig?”
- Cannot perform [bold][color=#004d75]Security Officer[/bold][/color] duties unless [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] is understaffed.
This does not apply on [bold][color=red]Code Red[/bold][/color] or higher.
- Can deputize a [bold][color=#004d75]Security Officer[/bold][/color] to act as a second [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color] if it is necessary.
The [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color] is entirely responsible for the [bold][color=#00527d]Deputized Officer[/bold][/color], and can be held liable for any SoP/Space Law violations the [bold][color=#00527d]Deputized Officer[/bold][/color] commits.
- Can carry a chosen longarm at all times, within reason.
- Is considered the [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color]’s Second in Command.
## Responsible For: - Defending the [bold][color=#004d75]Security Department[/bold][/color]. - Ensuring Contraband is stored properly within the Armory. - Ensuring the Armory is always stocked on materials and weaponry.
80 of each basic material is generally enough. If weapons go missing, replacements must be ordered. - Handing out Permits for Minor Contraband & [bold][color=#004d75]Security Equipment[/bold][/color]. See: [bold][textlink="Permit Acquisition" link="security-sop-permitacquisition"][/bold] - Ensuring [bold][textlink="Prisoner Treatment" link="security-sop-prisonertreatment"][/bold] is followed to the letter.
Neglecting this responsibility can lead to criminal punishment if the Prisoner was adversely affected or injured. - Sentencing Prisoners for crimes over 15 minutes. - Ensuring Prisoners are processed properly, changing statuses from Wanted to Detained, or Detained to Released as to prevent confusion for patrolling [bold][color=#004d75]Security Officers[/bold][/color]. - Ensuring Space Law & SoP is followed by [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color]. - Coordinating the [bold][color=#004d75]Security Department[/bold][/color], such as providing information on a suspect’s location or updating the Criminal Records computer when requested.
On [bold][color=#1E90FF]Code Blue[/bold][/color], permits for Longarms and additional Sidearms are allowed. Armor can be handed out without a permit.
On [bold][color=red]Code Red[/bold][/color] or higher, all [bold][color=#004d75]Security Equipment[/bold][/color] can be handed out to [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] without a permit. Lethals can also additionally be handed out to members of [bold][color=#e6b363]Command Staff[/bold][/color] with a limit of one per individual, so long as it does not interfere with [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color]’s ability to handle the threat.
On [bold][color=pink]Code Gamma[/bold][/color] all [bold][color=#004d75]Security Equipment[/bold][/color] can be handed out to the station at large with the [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color]’s permission without a permit.
Standard [bold][color=green]Code Green[/bold][/color] Equipment:
in
or
